news.title: Chandigarh to set up 17 more sites for construction waste disposal
news.para: Chandigarh Municipal Corporation is establishing 17 new dedicated sites for construction and demolition waste to address increasing generation. The civic body aims for at least one collection point per ward to facilitate responsible disposal and curb illegal dumping. An agency has been hired to construct these sites, with progress pending site allocation.

news.title: About 11 illegal buildings set to be razed in Bengaluru
news.para: Bengaluru East city corporation has commenced the demolition of 11 illegal structures found with significant deviations from approved building plans. A building in Whitefield with multiple unauthorized floors is currently being dismantled manually. This action follows a survey of over 600 under-construction buildings and the issuance of notices.

news.title: Bhubaneswar civic body plans to include 72 villages under its ambit
news.para: The Bhubaneswar Municipal Corporation plans to incorporate 72 revenue villages into its jurisdiction following consent from the villages. This expansion will see the administrative zones increase from three to six, bringing services like sanitation and water supply to the newly added areas.

news.title: Knowledge Realty Trust's net operating income up 20% in H1 FY26
news.para: Knowledge Realty Trust posted a robust 20% year-on-year net operating income growth to ₹19,544 million in H1 FY26, maintaining strong 89% margins. The company announced its first distribution of ₹6,900 million and successfully raised ₹16,000 million via NCDs, securing significant interest cost savings. Leasing activity remained strong, with portfolio occupancy reaching 92%.

news.title: Hubballi-Dharwad development body steps in to protect non-NA site owners from harassment
news.para: Hubballi-Dharwad Urban Development Authority (Huda) is surveying non-NA properties to protect residents facing demands from original landowners' heirs. Many have lived on these plots for decades, having purchased them through bond agreements. Huda aims to provide legal protection and prevent exploitation.

news.title: Mohali RWAs demand construction of road promised in master plan
news.para: Residents in Mohali's Sectors 123, 124, and 125 are demanding the construction of the 200ft Master Plan Road. They feel cheated as the promised road, crucial for connectivity, has not been built. This has led to severe traffic congestion and safety concerns on narrow internal roads. The residents plan further protests if work does not begin soon.

news.title: Saya Group clears ₹1,500 crore debt
news.para: Saya Group has successfully repaid around ₹1,500 crore in debt over the last five years, including term loans and NCDs from institutions like IIFL Finance and Yes Bank. This significant debt reduction highlights the company's strong financial health and efficient project delivery. With a strengthened balance sheet, Saya Group is now poised for future expansion and new ventures.

news.title: Goa: GIDC pitches 50% fee waiver for online building nods
news.para: Goa Industrial Development Corporation (GIDC) is launching a new initiative to boost digital adoption and revive defunct industrial units.  Starting November 15, a 50% waiver on construction licence fees will be offered for one year on applications submitted through the Online Building Plan Approval System (OBPAS). This aims to accelerate project clearances and encourage productive use of industrial land.

news.title: TCP must inform us of construction plans exceeding 20,000 sq meters: Goa-SEIAA
news.para: Goa's environmental authority now requires planning departments to report building plan approvals exceeding 20,000 sqm. This move aims to ensure projects have necessary environmental clearances before construction begins. The authority will also inform departments upon approving such projects, streamlining the verification process.

news.title: Navi Mumbai civic body appeals residents to conduct audit of buildings older than 30 years
news.para: Navi Mumbai Municipal Corporation declared 513 buildings dangerous, urging residents to conduct structural audits for buildings over 30 years old by March 31, 2026. Failure to comply, as mandated by the Maharashtra Municipal Corporation Act, can result in significant fines. Residents are strongly advised to vacate dangerous structures to prevent potential loss of life and property.

news.title: Unity SFB leads Aviom India Housing race with ₹775-crore bid
news.para: Authum Investment and Infrastructure, Northern Arc, DMI Housing, Kifs Housing Finance and Omkara Asset Reconstruction Company are among others in fray for the asset, said the people cited above. Authum has given an offer of Rs 750 crore, which includes Rs 450 upfront and remaining staggered over two years, while Northern Arc has offered Rs 625 crore, of which Rs 325 crore is upfront and the remaining will be paid in two years, said the people cited above.

news.title: Concrete plants not green, can’t have them in industrial estates: Goa Inc
news.para: Goa's industrial associations are raising alarms over Ready-Mix Concrete (RMC) plants in estates, citing dust and slurry spills that disrupt clean manufacturing, particularly for pharma and food units. Despite being green-categorized, these plants cause operational issues, prompting fears of relocation. Concerns were voiced at a GIDC meeting regarding indirect entry of RMC units.

news.title: Maharashtra: Only 25% slum rehabilitation goal met in three years
news.para: Maharashtra's slum rehabilitation program is significantly behind schedule, having completed only 25% of its 1.5 lakh tenement target for 2022-25. This slow pace jeopardizes the larger goal of constructing over 5 lakh houses by 2030. Experts point to cost constraints and suggest slum upgradation as a more viable alternative.

news.title: East Bengaluru residents decry G+5 buildings on 18-foot road, fear layout will collapse
news.para: Residents of Nanjappa Layout are protesting unauthorized G+5 apartment constructions on an 18-foot road, alleging builders are violating height and safety norms. Despite repeated complaints to the Greater Bengaluru Authority (GBA), no action has been taken, leading to concerns about choked infrastructure and overcrowding.

news.title: Adani Enterprises likely to pip Vedanta to emerge highest bidder for Jaiprakash Associates
news.para: In a strategic move, Adani Enterprises is set to take over Jaiprakash Associates Ltd., with an attractive acquisition offer that promises payment within two years, outshining Vedanta's five-year timeline. The Committee of Creditors has rated Adani's proposal the highest following a thorough evaluation of resolution plans.

news.title: Rajkot civic body crosses 50% of year’s property tax collection target
news.para: Rajkot Municipal Corporation has reached 50% of its annual property tax target, collecting Rs 301.03 crore. Incentives include rebates for early payment and additional discounts for online transactions. The RMC is also actively sealing properties of defaulters and utilizing WhatsApp for efficient bill delivery and payment.

news.title: Hubballi-Dharwad civic body project to boost mapping accuracy, earn ₹50 crore incentive
news.para: Hubballi-Dharwad Municipal Corporation is pioneering the establishment of its own high-precision GPS reference stations, a first for any civic body in India. This initiative, costing Rs 90 lakh, will enhance mapping and urban planning by providing accurate location data for civic services. The project also positions HDMC for a Rs 50 crore incentive.

news.title: Centre orders probe into PMAY housing project for CRZ violations in Kharghar
news.para: The Union environment ministry has directed Maharashtra to investigate alleged Coastal Regulation Zone (CRZ) violations in a Navi Mumbai PMAY housing project. Environmental groups complain the construction is too close to mangroves and the high-tide line, posing climate risks. The ministry asked the state coastal authority to examine the matter and take action if violations are confirmed.

news.title: Major relief for Delhi Development Authority in housing project case
news.para: Delhi's Real Estate Appellate Tribunal (REAT) overturned a Rs 1 crore penalty against the Delhi Development Authority (DDA) for failing to register a housing project. REAT ruled that the Rohini project was completed before RERA's establishment in 2017, making registration unnecessary. The tribunal sent the case back to RERA for a review of the complaint.
